title: Segfault when combining multiple years with combine

posts.name: Phillip Tyler Masterson
posts.text:
Hi all!  Recently, I’ve been running into an unusual error with combine.  I currently have four datacards that I would like to merge and run AsymptoticLimits on, located on lxplus at /eos/home-p/phmaster/tth_hqu/CMSSW_11_3_4/src/PlotToolsTTH/ttH/cards/scripts/runspuppi_sig_201*/ttHcc*.txt.  After combining the cards as follows:
combineCards.py puppi_sig_201*/ttHcc*.txt > ttHcc_SL.txt
I then run:
combine -M AsymptoticLimits -d ttHcc_SL.txt
This results in a segfault with no debugging message:
...
=> Total parameter prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBJ_CR_bin1[0.00,-7.00,7.00] to be gaussian constrained
------------------------------------------------------------
Channel ch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBB_CR will use autoMCStats with settings: event-threshold=0, include-signal=0, hist-mode=1
============================================================
Analysing bin errors for: prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBB_CR
Poisson cut-off: 0
Processes excluded for sums: ttH_hcc
============================================================
Bin        Contents        Error           Notes
0          693.184195      7.925550        total sum
0          693.026956      7.925549        excluding marked processes
0          7646.000000     87.441409       Unweighted events, alpha=0.090660
=> Total parameter prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBB_CR_bin0[0.00,-7.00,7.00] to be gaussian constrained
------------------------------------------------------------
1          357.465258      5.319881        total sum
1          357.436395      5.319881        excluding marked processes
1          4514.000000     67.186308       Unweighted events, alpha=0.079190
=> Total parameter prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBB_CR_bin1[0.00,-7.00,7.00] to be gaussian constrained
------------------------------------------------------------
Segmentation fault (core dumped)

I’ve noticed something highly unusual:  combining any two of the four datacards does not produce a segfault, while combining three or more does.  The issue does not involve any one card, only three or more together.  I thought that maybe this pointed to a memory issue, but I tried clearing out some old files and switching to a new lxplus node, and I’m still getting the same error.
Does anyone have any ideas about what’s going on, or how I can debug this when combine isn’t giving me any terminal output?
posts.cooked_html:
Hi all! Recently, I’ve been running into an unusual error with combine. I currently have four datacards that I would like to merge and run AsymptoticLimits on, located on lxplus at /eos/home-p/phmaster/tth_hqu/CMSSW_11_3_4/src/PlotToolsTTH/ttH/cards/scripts/runspuppi_sig_201*/ttHcc*.txt. After combining the cards as follows:

combineCards.py puppi_sig_201*/ttHcc*.txt > ttHcc_SL.txt

I then run:

combine -M AsymptoticLimits -d ttHcc_SL.txt

This results in a segfault with no debugging message:

...
=> Total parameter prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBJ_CR_bin1[0.00,-7.00,7.00] to be gaussian constrained
------------------------------------------------------------
Channel ch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBB_CR will use autoMCStats with settings: event-threshold=0, include-signal=0, hist-mode=1

Poisson cut-off: 0
Processes excluded for sums: ttH_hcc
============================================================

0          693.184195      7.925550        total sum                     
0          693.026956      7.925549        excluding marked processes    
0          7646.000000     87.441409       Unweighted events, alpha=0.090660
  => Total parameter prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBB_CR_bin0[0.00,-7.00,7.00] to be gaussian constrained
------------------------------------------------------------
1          357.465258      5.319881        total sum                     
1          357.436395      5.319881        excluding marked processes    
1          4514.000000     67.186308       Unweighted events, alpha=0.079190
  => Total parameter prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBB_CR_bin1[0.00,-7.00,7.00] to be gaussian constrained
------------------------------------------------------------
Segmentation fault (core dumped)

I’ve noticed something highly unusual: combining any two of the four datacards does not produce a segfault, while combining three or more does. The issue does not involve any one card, only three or more together. I thought that maybe this pointed to a memory issue, but I tried clearing out some old files and switching to a new lxplus node, and I’m still getting the same error.

Does anyone have any ideas about what’s going on, or how I can debug this when combine isn’t giving me any terminal output?

posts.name: Phillip Tyler Masterson
posts.text:
Update:  Running with -v 3 gives slightly more information, although there’s a large amount of extra text that gets written to the output.  The last section is:
...
prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBJ_CR_bin1,prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBB_CR_bin0,prop_binch4_ttHcc_SLm_catBB_CR_bin1)
observables:(CMS_th1x,CMS_channel)

generic objects
---------------
RooStats::ModelConfig::ModelConfig
RooStats::ModelConfig::ModelConfig_bonly
RooArgSet::discreteParams

Segmentation fault (core dumped)

posts.name: Huilin Qu
posts.text:
Hi @phmaster ,
Maybe you can try increasing the stack size with the command ulimit -s unlimited before running combine?
Best,
Huilin
posts.cooked_html:
Hi @phmaster ,

Maybe you can try increasing the stack size with the command ulimit -s unlimited before running combine?

Best,
Huilin

posts.name: Phillip Tyler Masterson
posts.text:
Huilin Qu:

ulimit -s unlimited

That got it working.  Thank you so much!
posts.cooked_html:
Huilin Qu:

ulimit -s unlimited

That got it working. Thank you so much!
